name: Surrozen, Inc./DE
business: Surrozen is a biotechnology company discovering and developing product candidates to selectively modulate the Wnt pathway, with a current strategic focus on ophthalmology. The company's lead product candidates are multi-specific antibody-based therapeutics that mimic naturally occurring Wnt proteins for serious eye diseases. Surrozen currently has no approved products and generates revenue from licensing and research collaborations.

exposures.sentence: Our lead product candidates are multi-specific, antibody-based therapeutics that mimic the roles of naturally occurring Wnt proteins, which are involved in activation and enhancement of the Wnt pathway.

catalysts.sentence: We anticipate submitting an IND for SZN-8141 in the second half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Second closing of 2025 PIPE contingent on FDA clearance of IND for SZN-8141
catalysts.sentence: The second tranche of the 2025 PIPE is contingent upon the public announcement of the receipt of clearance from the FDA on or prior to October 31, 2026 of our Investigation New Drug Application for SZN-8141.

tamClaims.claim: In the U.S., the prevalence of DR is almost 10 million people.
tamClaims.sentence: In the U.S., the prevalence of DR is almost 10 million people.

tamClaims.claim: DME occurs in approximately 15% of patients with DR, or almost 1.4 million people in the U.S.
tamClaims.sentence: DME occurs in approximately 15% of patients with DR, or almost 1.4 million people in the U.S.

tamClaims.claim: Wet AMD is estimated to occur in approximately 1.3 million people in the U.S.
tamClaims.sentence: Wet AMD is estimated to occur in approximately 1.3 million people in the U.S.

tamClaims.claim: In the U.S., estimates range widely from 80,000 to 168,000 cases of uveitis each year.
tamClaims.sentence: In the U.S., estimates range widely from 80,000 to 168,000 cases of uveitis each year.

tamClaims.claim: It is estimated that up to 30% of all uveitis patients may develop macular edema, or UME.
tamClaims.sentence: It is estimated that up to 30% of all uveitis patients may develop macular edema, or UME.

tamClaims.claim: Branded anti-VEGF therapies for the treatment of retinal diseases, including wet AMD and DME, cumulatively generated over $13 billion in global revenue in 2025.
tamClaims.sentence: Branded anti-VEGF therapies for the treatment of retinal diseases, including wet AMD and DME, cumulatively generated over $13 billion in global revenue in 2025.
